title: Amplification and Pooling
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ecgei","text":"This protocol describes the amplification procedure of dual-indexed double-stranded DNA libraries, for shotgun Illumina sequencing. It is typically used for libraries indexed using the following protocol: (https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bakticwn)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":206,"length":49}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"AFA0B5521A8E11EB86BB0A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bakticwn"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"77fmp","text":"Working in an Molecular Biology Laboratory","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":42}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6j85f","text":"This protocol can place in a typical DNA-based molecular biology lab.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3qas1","text":"Please keep in mind the safety guidelines of your specific country and institution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"96hm5","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"272r0","text":"Recommendations include wearing of:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7sbmb","text":"- lab coats","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4dfgh","text":"- closed shoes and trousers","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8gu3g","text":"- safety glasses","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"akkro","text":"- nitril or latex gloves","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"avfq8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Prepare amplification assay [ 100 µL, per reaction ] Based on the quantification results of the indexed libaries ( https://dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bakticwn ) calculate the number of PCR cycles (amplification factor) needed to reach 10^13 copies of DNA per indexed Library. Formula in Excel to get the Cycles needed =LOG((1*10^13/Copies per rxn),2) (log base 2) Example: The following calculation is for 5 µL per reaction, with two indexed library samples (A and B) having different concentrations of DNA copies. Optonial Changes: 1.If the Calculation shows up less then 3 Cycles, you also have the Option to add less then 5 µl. 2. Instead of 4 reactions of 5 µl each you can also split in 8 reactions of 2 µl each Instead of 4 x 5 µl reactions you can also split in 8 x 2 µl reactions %s %s Do not calculate the amount of cycles for a higher amount of copies than 1.4 *10^13 to avoid heteroduplexes.

Amplify in a thermocycler with the following program: %s %s Adjust the number of cycles according to the amplification factor as calculated in step 1. During this incubation take MinElute columns out of the fridge so they warm up to room temperature before use in the next step. This is an ideal point to prepare downstream steps, including labelling of final elution tubes, MinElute Columns etc.
Purify with MinElute kit with the following modifications to the manufacturer's protocol: Use one column for all four reactions [= 400 µL PCR product] of a sample.

Dilute amplified index library 1:10 with HPLC- water and check for fragment size, concentration, and heteroduplexes. (for example with the D1000 Kit's Tape, Reagent and Buffer - following the manufacturer's protocol on the TapeStation) if you see heteroduplexes you need to perform a reconditoning PCR. Reconditoning PCR: one cycle PCR using 100 ng library template in a 100 µl Herculase PCR reaction (same set up as in 3) and amplified with 1 cycles of 95°C for 2 min, 58°C for 2 min, and 72°C for 5 min. Purify with MinElute kit following the instructions from Step 5, but elute in 20µl EBT.

Dilute each amplified indexed library to 10 nM with EBT buffer or HPLC-water for shotgun sequencing. Then pool the 10 nM amplified indexed libraries in equimolar amounts (take the same volume for each sample). The final concentration of a pool of several amplfied indexed libraries should be 10 nM .
Set up four amplification reactions of 100 µL each per library %s %s
Vortex master mix before adding the enzyme. After adding the enzyme, mix by pipetting or inverting the tube.
Pipette 95 µL mastermix and 5 µL indexed library into each tube (use 0.2 mL PCR strips). Keep the remaining library at -20 °C until further use.

Add 2400 µL PB or PBI* buffer to a 5 mL tube for each sample (this is 600 µL buffer for each PCR reaction). Add all 4 PCR reactions per sample to the same tube with PB buffer and vortex briefly. After the PCR product is mixed with the PBI, the PBI should keep its yellow colour. If it turns purple the pH is too high and the efficency of the MinElute columns is not guaranteed.
Add 700 µL PE (wash) buffer, spin 15800 x g, °C, 1 minute , and discard flow-through.

Add 50 µL EBT buffer to the center of the filter, incubate for 00:02:00 , and spin 15800 x g, °C, 1 minute to elute the amplified indexed library. Carefully pipette EBT directly onto the center of the membrane without touching the membrane.
Repeat loading until the complete mixture was run through the column. go to step #5.2

Load 700 µL of the mixture onto one MinElute column, incubate for 00:02:00 , spin 15800 x g, °C, 1 minute , and discard flow-through. Pour off the liquid into a waste tube, and pat the rim of the collection tube dry on a paper tissue or towel. Use just one spot on the paper tissue per sample. Be careful not to touch the rim of the tube on the waste container. After you are finished with all samples, discard the paper and wipe clean the surface underneath with water and soap.
Put column into new 1.5 mL LoBind tubes.

Check the 10 nM library or the 10 nM library pool for the correct concentration, (for example with the HighSensitivity D1000 Kit's Tape, Reagent and Buffer following the manufacturer's protocol on the TapeStation.)
Prepare cleaned workspace with all necessary reagents and equipment. Label all 0.2 mL PCR strips for the PCR reactions.
creator: Franziska Aron
